name: hexamine stove
description: Known as an Esbit stove, this is a lightweight, folding stove designed to use small hexamine tablets for cooking.

name: mess tin
description: A compact military-style pan and tray, designed for heating food over a fire or portable stove.  It is shallower than a proper pot or pan, and lacks the integrated heating elements modern mess kits have.

name: camp stove
description: This is a single burner butane camp stove, with a small piezoletric igniter on the dial regulator, and stands for cookware.  A compartment on the side holds the butane canister, and a lever depresses the check valve when in use.  Use in a well ventilated area unless you like carbon monoxide poisoning.

name: camp stove
description: This is a folding metal stand for cookware with a burner and a hose, with an adapter for lareger propane tanks.  This seems like a great propane accessory for backpackers.
